semanticCore: Injustice, depriving rights, digesting, hollow, soft, or narrow
primaryMeaning: Depriving rights/injustice or soft/hollow date spathes
quranicRelevance: Fearing no deprivation of rights (Hadhm - 'wa-la hadhman'), and soft date spathes ('hadheemun').

keyDerivations.meaning: Deprivation of rights, injustice, deficit, decrease, or diminution
Soft, clustered, hollow, easily digested, or layered spathes

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَمَنْ يَعْمَلْ مِنَ الصَّالِحَاتِ وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ فَلَا يَخَافُ ظُلْمًا وَلَا هَضْمًا (20:112): And whoever does of righteous deeds while he is a believer - he will fear neither injustice nor deprivation of rights (Hadhm).
discardedNoise: Stomach food digestion (Hadhm) in medical guides.
summaryEn: The root represents deprivation of rights and soft structures (Hadhm), assuring believers of complete justice in the Hereafter and describing Paradise date clusters.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر البخس وهضم الحقوق والكسر اللين المتراكم (هضم)، واعداً المؤمن بألا ينقص من حسناته شيء (ولا هضماً)، وواصفاً نضارة طلع النخيل.
rawText: إِهْتَضَمَ 8 see 1. === اِهْتَضَمَ الشَّجَزَ مِنْ أَعَلاَهُ (K voce شَعَبَ) He cropped the tops of the shrubs: see شَعَبَ. اِنْهَضَمَ 7 It (a thing) melted, or dissolved, after being congealed. (JK.) === اِنْهَضَمَتِ الثَّمَرَةُ, and 🡻تَهَضَّمَت, The fruit broke; or became broken, or crushed; syn. تَشَدَّخَتْ; (TA;) and became mellow, so as to be easy of digestion. See also بَوْعٌ === اِنْهَضَمَ, said of food, (MA), It was, or became, digestible, or easy of digestion. (MA, KL.) === اِنْهضَامٌ of the زَوْر [app. Depression]. (K in art. جنف. [There coupled with دُخُولٌ.]) See also صَقَلٌ. تَهَضَّمَ 5 see 7. هضَمَهُ 1 and : see تَخَوَّفَهُ. === هَضَمَ لَهُ مِنْ مَالِهِ: see خَضَمَ. هَاضُومٌ Any medicine [or other thing (see حَاطُومٌ)] that is a digestive of food; as also 🡻هَضُومٌ; (K;) i. q. جَوَارِشٌ. (S.) هَضَمٌ Contraction of the sides, (S, K,) and lankness of the belly, and smallness of the flank: (K:) in a horse it is a fault. (S.) هَضُومٌ : see هَاضُومٌ.
